behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at a module boundary (request builder -> API client payload), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: a real network call is not required; an in-process API stub that captures outbound payload can expose missing/partial taxonomy lists. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is only about request content, not model judgment or multi-step agent flow.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier module to a stub provider client, invoke classification with a known taxonomy, and assert the outbound request includes the full expected label list (no omissions/reordering bugs from wiring/serialization).

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is response handoff/validation across provider adapter and classifier output boundary. A unit test of a mapper could miss envelope parsing/wiring defects; a stubbed provider response through the module catches real boundary failures. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real HTTP or real model quality, only handling of an out-of-taxonomy returned value.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub provider that returns a valid response envelope containing an invalid label, call the public classify path, and assert the returned label is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is explicitly one-function deterministic normalization given identical input; no cross-module interaction is needed to trigger the failure. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none apply because inputs are fixed raw responses and output determinism is local logic. Rejected higher levels: text disqualifies them by constraining to identical raw responses and the normalization step itself.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization repeatedly with the same raw response fixture(s) and assert identical normalized output every time (including case/whitespace variants represented in fixture set).

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls; execution path depends on real model/provider behavior. Deterministic fixtures would hide failures tied to real provider variability and real returned content, so Integration is too low. Rejected Workflow: calls are repeated instances of a single agentic decision, not a multi-stage agent pipeline with chained model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Run N real classifications for the same document/taxonomy, record raw provider label and final normalized label per call, and assert final label only changes when raw provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure usually occurs where config, tokenizer/counting logic, and dispatch gating interact; testing only one function can miss handoff bugs. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network is required because this behavior is explicitly pre-dispatch. Rejected higher levels: text scopes to construction-time enforcement, not live provider/model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Configure a small budget and exercise over-budget inputs through the public classify path with a stub provider; assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) happens before any outbound dispatch call.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real AI provider call and real provider-reported usage metadata; mocks can mask true tokenizer/completion behavior and produce false confidence. Per LLM-DEP caution, even deterministic assertions can require Agentic when the behavior depends on real AI API return characteristics. Rejected System as final: behavior is not just HTTP transport correctness; it depends on model/provider token accounting tied to actual model execution.
behaviors.test_description: Execute real classification calls on representative documents, capture provider-reported prompt/completion/total tokens, and assert totals are within configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: retry correctness is at orchestration boundary (error handling + backoff + retry + success propagation). A stub provider can reliably return rate-limit then success and expose real logic failures without real network. Rejected System/Agentic: behavior text is about control flow on rate-limit signals, not real model output quality or OS-only effects.
behaviors.test_description: With a stub provider scripted to return rate-limit error then success, call classify and assert: at least one wait/backoff occurs, a retry is attempted, and caller receives success instead of immediate error.
